comment_text.sn35.131:1.4: The remainder of the text is identical to SN 35.118.

root_text.sn35.131:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye.
root_text.sn35.131:1.2: Atha kho nakulapitā gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho nakulapitā gahapati bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.131:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti?
root_text.sn35.131:1.4: Ko pana, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti?
root_text.sn35.131:1.5: “Santi kho, gahapati, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.131:1.6: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.131:1.7: Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.131:1.10: santi kho, gahapati, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.131:1.11: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.131:1.12: Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.131:1.14: Ayaṁ kho, gahapati, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti.
root_text.sn35.131:2.1: Santi ca kho, gahapati, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.131:2.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.131:2.3: Tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato na tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti, na tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.131:2.6: santi kho, gahapati, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.131:2.7: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.131:2.8: Tassa taṁ nābhinandato nābhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato na tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti na tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.131:2.10: Ayaṁ kho, gahapati, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.131:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Bhaggas at Crocodile’s Bellow, in the deer park at Bhesakaḷā’s Wood.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.2: Then the householder Nakula’s father went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn35.131:1.3: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life?
translation_text.sn35.131:1.4: What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life?”
translation_text.sn35.131:1.5: “Householder, there are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.6: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.131:1.7: their consciousness has that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.8: A mendicant with fuel for grasping does not become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.9: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.131:1.10: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.11: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.131:1.12: their consciousness has that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.13: A mendicant with fuel for grasping does not become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.131:1.14: That’s the cause, that’s the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.1: There are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.2: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.131:2.3: their consciousness doesn’t have that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.4: A mendicant free of grasping becomes extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.5: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.131:2.6: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.7: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.131:2.8: their consciousness doesn’t have that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.9: A mendicant free of grasping becomes extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.131:2.10: That’s the cause, that’s the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life.”
